This standard concessionary charge applies to the first time and normal data access request. In other cases, the Bank reserves the right to charge the actual commercial cost incurred without applying a cap to the charge. In any case, the Bank will inform the data requestor individually the actual handling charge and will only process the request upon receiving the requestor's acceptance.

If you ask us to set up a standing instruction to pay from your account, which if executed by us, would cause your account to go overdrawn or over an existing overdraft limit, we will treat this as your ad-hoc request for an unauthorised overdraft. Alternatively, we may refuse your request and your instruction.'; CELL_BOUNDARY_BLEED: 'Waived' appears as 'Wai | ved' in the Standing instructions Via HSBC Branches row (split across cells); CELL_BOUNDARY_BLEED: 'HK$150 per transaction' appears as 'HK$150 per tr | ansaction' in the Special payment requests row

partial_detail.note: Main table cell data is largely correct; errors are concentrated in footnote section and two CELL_BOUNDARY_BLEED rows (rows 13 and 14)

pdf_footnotes.1: This standard concessionary charge applies to the first time and normal data access request. In other cases, the Bank reserves the right to charge the actual commercial cost incurred without applying a cap to the charge. In any case, the Bank will inform the data requestor individually the actual handling charge and will only process the request upon receiving the requestor's acceptance.
pdf_footnotes.2: If you ask us to set up a standing instruction to pay from your account, which if executed by us, would cause your account to go overdrawn or over an existing overdraft limit, we will treat this as your ad-hoc request for an unauthorised overdraft. We may agree to your request and provide you with an overdraft. The amount of the unauthorised overdraft will be subject to our prevailing interest rate calculated on a daily basis. We will also charge a handling charge for the unauthorised overdraft on each occasion. Alternatively, we may refuse your request and your instruction.
pdf_footnotes.3: Waived for senior citizens aged 65 and above / Senior Citizen Card holders

md_footnotes:
1 Applicable to all types of cheques including paper cheque and e-Cheque.
2 If you issue a cheque which, if honoured by us, would either cause your account to go overdrawn or over an existing overdraft limit, we will treat this as your ad-hoc request for an unauthorised overdraft. We may agree to your request and provide you with an overdraft.
The amount of the unauthorised overdraft will be subject to our prevailing interest rate calculated on a daily basis. We will also charge a handling charge for the unauthorised overdraft on each occasion. Alternatively, we may refuse your request, return the cheque to you and
impose a return charge for considering and refusing your request on each occasion.
3 This charge is made for the amount of a single cheque issued and presented in Guangdong Province (including Shenzhen) exceeds RMB80,000.
4 If you issue a cheque which contains an error or if your cheque is not issued in accordance with our requirements, we will treat this as your informal request for special service. We may refuse your request, return the cheque to you and charge, without prior notice,
a handling fee for considering your request on each occasion.
5 If you issue a cheque from your RMB Current Account which, if honoured by us, would either cause your account to go overdrawn, you will need to request for fund transfer from RMB Savings Account to RMB Current Account. If you do not make a request for fund transfer,
we will treat this as your informal request for fund transfer and carry out the fund transfer. In either case, we may charge, without prior notice, a handling fee for considering your request.
6 As quoted by HSBC from time to time
.
label: INCORRECT
error_reason: MISSING_FOOTNOTE_NUM: Footnote 4 marker is absent from the sentence beginning 'If you issue a cheque which contains an error...' — the markdown renders this as a plain unlabelled paragraph instead of '4 If you issue a cheque which contains an error...'; TRAILING_ARTIFACT: Footnote 6 ends with a stray period on a new line ('6 As quoted by HSBC from time to time\n.') — the period is separated onto its own line instead of being part of the sentence

partial_detail.note: Main table cell data is fully correct; errors are limited to footnote section only

pdf_footnotes.1: Applicable to all types of cheques including paper cheque and e-Cheque.
pdf_footnotes.2: If you issue a cheque which, if honoured by us, would either cause your account to go overdrawn or over an existing overdraft limit, we will treat this as your ad-hoc request for an unauthorised overdraft. We may agree to your request and provide you with an overdraft. The amount of the unauthorised overdraft will be subject to our prevailing interest rate calculated on a daily basis. We will also charge a handling charge for the unauthorised overdraft on each occasion. Alternatively, we may refuse your request, return the cheque to you and impose a return charge for considering and refusing your request on each occasion.
pdf_footnotes.3: This charge is made for the amount of a single cheque issued and presented in Guangdong Province (including Shenzhen) exceeds RMB80,000.
pdf_footnotes.4: If you issue a cheque which contains an error or if your cheque is not issued in accordance with our requirements, we will treat this as your informal request for special service. We may refuse your request, return the cheque to you and charge, without prior notice, a handling fee for considering your request on each occasion.
pdf_footnotes.5: If you issue a cheque from your RMB Current Account which, if honoured by us, would either cause your account to go overdrawn, you will need to request for fund transfer from RMB Savings Account to RMB Current Account. If you do not make a request for fund transfer, we will treat this as your informal request for fund transfer and carry out the fund transfer. In either case, we may charge, without prior notice, a handling fee for considering your request.
pdf_footnotes.6: As quoted by HSBC from time to time.

pdf_footnotes.3: If you instruct us to make a payment from your account by autoPay, which if executed by us, would either cause your account to go overdrawn or over an existing overdraft limit, we will treat this as your ad-hoc request for an unauthorised overdraft.
pdf_footnotes.4: Includes inward payments from overseas bank accounts irrespective of routing arrangement.
pdf_footnotes.5: Not applicable to the charges option selected to 'our'.
pdf_footnotes.6: For Hong Kong dollar cheques drawn on banks in Guangdong Province including Shenzhen, a flat fee of HK$50 per cheque will be charged.
pdf_footnotes.7: Plus correspondent bank and/or overseas bank charges if applicable.
pdf_footnotes.8: Excluding Hang Seng Bank Limited and its subsidiaries.
pdf_footnotes.9: Waived for senior citizens aged 65 and above / Senior Citizen Card holders.

partial_detail.note: The additional fee row and amendment/cancellation row have correct charge values but wrong column structure; all rows suffer from column collapse

pdf_footnotes.note_1: For telegraphic transfer sending direct to or routing through Australia, Bangladesh, Canada, Mainland China, France, India, Indonesia, Japan, Korea South, Macau SAR, Malaysia, Mauritius, New Zealand, Philippines, Singapore, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Thailand, UK, USA and Vietnam, an optional flat fee of HK$150 can be made to cover the first payment charges imposed by overseas HSBC banks/branches for their processing. If the telegraphic transfer is made through HSBC Internet Banking or HSBC Mobile Banking, the flat fee is HK$50.
pdf_footnotes.note_2: Outward Payments include payments to overseas bank accounts irrespective of routing arrangement.
pdf_footnotes.note_3: Please note that some overseas banks may impose additional overseas charges. At the overseas banks' discretion, the charge may be deducted from the payment amount.
pdf_footnotes.note_4: Additional charges may be levied by other banks where incorrect, incomplete or unclear information is provided by customers in the instructions.

pdf_footnotes.1: The monthly below balance fee applies to account held by HSBC Jade client.
pdf_footnotes.2: The below balance fee is imposed based on your Total Relationship Balance (TRB).
pdf_footnotes.3: Excluding Hang Seng Bank Limited and its subsidiaries.
pdf_footnotes.4: Applicable to telegraphic transfers to self-named HSBC accounts (including joint accounts with identical account holders).
pdf_footnotes.5: It refers to the flat fee of HK$150 covering the overseas HSBC banks/branches payment charges.
pdf_footnotes.6: Other additional overseas charges (if applicable) will be borne by customers.
pdf_footnotes.7: Funds transferred through RTGS will normally be credited to the beneficiary's account on the transfer date.
pdf_footnotes.8: Please note that some beneficiary banks may impose additional charges.
pdf_footnotes.9: Participant means a participant of FPS.
pdf_footnotes.10: If your statement balance exceeds the existing credit limit on your card, we will treat this as your informal request for raising your credit limit.
pdf_footnotes.11: Applicable to the HSBC Jade client's Premier Accounts only.
pdf_footnotes.12: If you pay or withdraw from your account which would cause your account to go overdrawn or over an existing overdraft limit, we will treat this as your ad-hoc request for an unauthorised overdraft.

pdf_footnotes.1: For HSBC Premier/HSBC One/Personal Integrated Account clients, surcharges of overseas banks may be levied (if applicable). For HSBC Jade clients, the surcharges of overseas banks will be refunded if levied.
pdf_footnotes.2: Any withdrawal of one of the 12 supported currencies through overseas ATMs will be debited from your respective foreign currency account, if you have sufficient foreign currency.
pdf_footnotes.3: If you withdraw in a currency other than the 12 supported ones, the amount is converted into HKD using the Mastercard rate.
pdf_footnotes.4: Foreign currency transaction fee is incurred when a transaction is effected in currencies other than Hong Kong dollars. This fee is waived for HSBC Mastercard Debit Card.
pdf_footnotes.5: Customers may sometimes be offered the option to settle foreign currency transactions in HKD at point of sale/ATM overseas.
